title: WebNami vs Bear Blog — More Features, Still Minimal
description: Bear Blog is minimal and fast, but hosted-only with limited customization. WebNami is self-hosted, open source, and gives you a full admin with the same simplicity.

hero.heading: Minimal blogging with more control than Bear Blog
hero.subheading: Bear Blog's appeal is simplicity and speed — WebNami shares those values and goes further. You get a full admin dashboard, custom theming, and complete content ownership, all without the hosted-only constraints of Bear Blog.

reasons.heading: Truly self-hosted
reasons.body: Bear Blog is hosted-only — your content lives on their servers. WebNami runs locally, and you deploy static files anywhere you choose.

reasons.heading: Real theme control
reasons.body: Bear Blog lets you add custom CSS but that's it. WebNami uses Tailwind CSS v4 with full Nunjucks templating — build any look you want from scratch.

reasons.heading: A proper admin dashboard
reasons.body: Bear Blog's editor is intentionally bare-bones. WebNami includes a full admin with post management, page management, settings, and media uploads.

reasons.heading: Your files, not their servers
reasons.body: With Bear Blog your posts are stored on someone else's database. WebNami keeps everything as local Markdown files under your full control.
